reasons: Tigreal can group enemies into one fight area, giving Alice a strong entry point for AoE pressure.
Alice can follow Tigreal's control window with Crimson Gleam entry and Throne of Ruin disruption.

proof.summary: Tigreal groups and stuns enemies, while Alice enters the cluster to apply AoE damage, sustain, and control.
worksBestWhen: Alice has her entry and main fight tools ready.
The fight happens around an objective or chokepoint.
failureCases: Enemies spread out before Tigreal engages.
Alice arrives after the control window ends.
Enemy burst or anti-heal cuts down Alice's sustain after entry.

reasons: Atlas can pull multiple enemies together, creating a grouped target zone for Alice's AoE follow-up.
Alice can use the engage window to enter and pressure several enemies at once.

proof.summary: Atlas creates a multi-target engage, and Alice follows into the pulled area with sustained AoE pressure.
worksBestWhen: Atlas catches several enemies in the engage.
Alice positions close enough to follow instantly.
The team focuses the grouped targets after Alice enters.
failureCases: Atlas catches only one low-value target.
Alice enters before Atlas confirms the pull.
Enemy disengage breaks the teamfight formation.

reasons: Khufra can control enemy movement and punish dash-based escape attempts near Alice's fight zone.
Alice benefits when enemies are kept near her AoE pressure instead of freely repositioning.

proof.summary: Khufra disrupts enemy movement with knock-up, wall-slam, and anti-mobility pressure, while Alice follows with AoE sustain and control.
worksBestWhen: Khufra engages near terrain or a contested objective.
Alice follows during Khufra's control window.
Enemy heroes rely on movement skills to escape Alice's zone.
failureCases: There is no useful terrain for the engage.
Khufra uses his control defensively before Alice enters.
Enemies avoid grouping around Alice's fight area.

reasons: Angela can attach to Alice and shield her during a deep engage.
Alice can use the added protection to stay inside the enemy formation and continue applying AoE pressure.

proof.summary: Alice commits into dangerous fight zones, and Angela can shield and support her during that deep-entry window.
worksBestWhen: Angela attaches before Alice commits into the enemy formation.
Alice enters with key cooldowns ready.
The team follows while Alice draws pressure.
failureCases: Alice enters before Angela can attach.
Enemy burst breaks the shield before Alice stabilizes.
Alice dives beyond follow-up damage and is isolated after the shield ends.

reasons: Floryn can support Alice during deep engages because Bloom can heal allied heroes regardless of their map position.
Alice benefits from global healing when she repositions into the enemy backline or fights beyond normal support range.

proof.summary: Alice may dive beyond normal support range, while Floryn can still stabilize her with global healing and support value during the commit.
worksBestWhen: Floryn saves Bloom for Alice's real engage or objective fight.
Alice has enough HP and cooldowns to keep fighting after the heal lands.
The team times follow-up around Alice's deep pressure.
failureCases: Alice is burst before Bloom can stabilize her.
Floryn uses Bloom before Alice commits.
Anti-heal or heavy burst reduces the sustain value.
